item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, if you find a good cop in this world, you take 'em.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"and both subjects were offered positions in Foundation security after showing proficiency in responding to anomalous events and maintaining rational thought during a crisis."

Got my +1 in this moment. Love to see a caring foundation :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad! Originally there was another log before that one that was a lot darker, but I decided against including it and I think it was the right call. Nothin' wrong with upbeat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Post-interview, the intact SCP-5266-2 instance was retrieved from Subject A, and both subjects were offered positions in Foundation security after showing proficiency in responding to anomalous events and maintaining rational thought during a crisis.

Somehow this made me happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, that contrast between the Foundation and the demon thingy trying to be all serious and she sheer weirdness of the anomaly itself is what I was going for, I'm so glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can say with confidence that this is one of the most absurd things I have ever read.
I can also say with confidence that this is some of the most fun I have ever had while reading an SCP article.
I particularly enjoyed how you were able to maintain objectivity and seriousness in spite of the sheer nonsensicality of the subject. I don't know how you were able to suspend my disbelief in the documentation of the singing shrunken heads of the Village People, but you did it.
+1.
I'd upvote again if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last log was great! (decided to read it with YMCA in the background, was fun)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: haha i love that the foundation disliked the video showed in that first image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad I was able to brighten your day, even if just a little bit!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been knee deep in depressing SCP's, ones that fill me with existential dread and stick with me all day. So, at first I wasn't going to upvote this. But I came back, read again and I am glad I did. It helped as a nice cleanser from some of the heavy articles I've been revisiting and I think my knee-jerk neutral response was my brain not being ready for this.

It is one of the few SCiPs I would dare classify as cute. I mean, other than all the toxic death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, the heads only ever correspond to the village people. That would be funny, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Atleast until they realise that one of those uncontained singing heads from now on will be of a demonic prince. That might be troublesome in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, forgot to do that. I've been away for awhile so I've forgotten all the rituals and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haldings should get Employee of the Month for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I added the wikiwalk footer, but I love this! Easy upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks dude, glad you like it! I'm also impressed by how you, like, ascended while I was gone. Good stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it! Wonderful weirdness, a strong upvote. And I missed your work, Ooga :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That second log, though.

+1

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Then I have done my job well, I very much agree that people shouldn't be afraid to get weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Follow your dreams, write whatever you want my dude! There were no specific SCPs I had in mind, I just needed a conceivable reason for demonic presence to be at unsafe levels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
remarkable work. +1.

kinda want to write an article for one of those SCPs you mentioned in the last testing log and expand on whatever the hell vorth-ta'ag is. but i also don't want to take away from the excellent story and humor here.
The extremely weird side of the foundation is usually under-explored in the non-joke articles, so its pleasing to see this as well-written as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, thanks! I was busy adjusting to my first year and a half of college, but now that I've got things figured out I will hopefully be around more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, good to keep in mind. They were shorter because at first there was another log before the other two, so I wanted to keep the whole thing from being too long. However, I deleted that log without thinking about the dire consequences that would follow.

Edit: I just realized they got squished down when I copied them over. Fixing now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The one thing keeping me from confidently upvoting is the formatting of the logs. It's very compressed, you should probably try spacing it out a bit more. Otherwise, this was thoroughly enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I missed your work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really fantastic — succeeds on virtually every level. Absolutely nails the clinical tone. Well done.
Additional idea/riff: maybe this is why Vine got shut down — to contain an extremely-virulent 10-second version of this object.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Juxtaposition~!

Thanks to Mooagain, RadioactiveRADS, Cope, Topazshadow, Alongname, and Jabobai for critique and feedback. They helped me refine the idea and come up with the final log. The picture is mine, of me body-slamming the floor out of commitment to art.

Anyways, always remember: No matter how seriously the Foundation or anyone else takes itself, it is still always possible for something ridiculous to come out of nowhere to dunk on you. Because anomalies are, by their very definition, weird. And I like weird. Not to mention how fun it was trying to write action sequences set to Village People music, I hope I was able to translate timing-based jokes to a written format well enough.

I hope you all enjoy!
